source_paper_title: Two Typical Implementable Semismooth* Newton Methods for Generalized Equations Are G-Semismooth Newton Methods
context:
Section 6 (Conclusions), page 20

Accordingly, a natural question is whether an implementable semismooth* Newton method is achievable for solving a GE that cannot be reformulated to locally Lipschitz continuous equations.
title: Implementable semismooth\(^*\) Newton method for generalized equations not reducible to locally Lipschitz equations
background_latex:
Let \(X,Y\) be finite-dimensional real Hilbert spaces. A generalized equation (GE) is the inclusion
\[0\in \Phi(x),\]
where \(\Phi: X\rightrightarrows Y\) is a set-valued mapping with closed graph. A (local) reformulation of the GE as a nonsmooth equation means finding a single-valued mapping \(H:U\to Y\) on a neighborhood \(U\subseteq X\) such that:
- \(H\) is locally Lipschitz continuous on \(U\).
- The solution sets coincide locally: \(x\in U\) satisfies \(0\in\Phi(x)\) if and only if \(H(x)=0\).
An implementable semismooth\(^*\) Newton method refers to a semismooth\(^*\)-type Newton scheme for solving \(0\in\Phi(x)\) whose per-iteration subproblem is computationally tractable (e.g., reduces to solving linear systems or otherwise explicitly computable steps), as in existing implementations for special GEs that admit locally Lipschitz equation localizations.
problem_definition_latex: Design an implementable semismooth\(^*\) Newton method for solving a generalized equation \(0\in \Phi(x)\) in the case where there does not exist any local reformulation around a solution \(\bar x\) into a locally Lipschitz continuous equation \(H(x)=0\) (on a neighborhood of \(\bar x\)) that is locally equivalent to the GE.
latex:
Let \(X,Y\) be finite-dimensional real Hilbert spaces. A generalized equation (GE) is the inclusion
\[0\in \Phi(x),\]
where \(\Phi: X\rightrightarrows Y\) is a set-valued mapping with closed graph. A (local) reformulation of the GE as a nonsmooth equation means finding a single-valued mapping \(H:U\to Y\) on a neighborhood \(U\subseteq X\) such that:
- \(H\) is locally Lipschitz continuous on \(U\).
- The solution sets coincide locally: \(x\in U\) satisfies \(0\in\Phi(x)\) if and only if \(H(x)=0\).
An implementable semismooth\(^*\) Newton method refers to a semismooth\(^*\)-type Newton scheme for solving \(0\in\Phi(x)\) whose per-iteration subproblem is computationally tractable (e.g., reduces to solving linear systems or otherwise explicitly computable steps), as in existing implementations for special GEs that admit locally Lipschitz equation localizations.

Design an implementable semismooth\(^*\) Newton method for solving a generalized equation \(0\in \Phi(x)\) in the case where there does not exist any local reformulation around a solution \(\bar x\) into a locally Lipschitz continuous equation \(H(x)=0\) (on a neighborhood of \(\bar x\)) that is locally equivalent to the GE.
open_problem_quote: Accordingly, a natural question is whether an implementable semismooth* Newton method is achievable for solving a GE that cannot be reformulated to locally Lipschitz continuous equations.
